met_criteria: ALP elevated — value 260 documented (likely >2x ULN, assuming ULN ~120 IU/L) [p32]
Abdominal pain present [p32]
Patient age 64 years — consistent with adult criteria [p31]
missing_criteria: GGT result required — P2 obstructive picture criterion requires elevated ALP >2x ULN TOGETHER WITH elevated GGT (+/- raised bilirubin +/- raised ALT); GGT not documented [p32]
ALT and bilirubin results not documented — needed to complete the obstructive LFT picture assessment [p32]
If pursuing P3 pathway instead: ALP >2x ULN with elevated GGT confirming liver origin AND ALP must persist on re-testing after 3 months — no repeat result documented [p32]
result.notes: The local ULN for ALP is typically 110–120 IU/L in adult males, making ALP 260 approximately 2.2x ULN — this would meet the >2x ULN threshold. However, the P2 criterion explicitly requires ALP >2x ULN TOGETHER WITH elevated GGT; GGT is mandatory to confirm the obstructive picture and liver origin. Without GGT, neither the P2 nor the P3 pathway is fully met. If GGT is elevated, the referral would proceed at P2 (within 2 weeks). Note: generalised abdominal pain alone with abnormal LFTs in a 64-year-old Māori male also warrants consideration of underlying malignancy — if strong clinical suspicion of malignancy persists after LFT workup, the CT CAP pathway (p10) may be relevant. The CT CAP pathway for males >50 with strong suspicion of malignancy, unexplained weight loss >5% over 3–6 months, and no focal pathology could also be explored if those features are present.
result.suggested_wording: 64 yo Māori male presenting with generalised abdominal pain for 2 weeks. LFTs show obstructive picture: ALP 260 IU/L (>2x ULN), GGT [value] IU/L (elevated), ALT [value] IU/L, bilirubin [value] umol/L. Requesting urgent ultrasound abdomen to investigate obstructive liver pathology.
add_to_note: Add GGT result (and ALT, bilirubin if available) — e.g. 'GGT [value] IU/L, ALT [value] IU/L, bilirubin [value] umol/L'.
If GGT is elevated alongside ALP >2x ULN, document: 'Obstructive picture on LFTs: ALP 260 (>2x ULN) with elevated GGT, consistent with P2 criteria for urgent ultrasound abdomen.'
If GGT is normal, a repeat LFT at 3 months is required before the P3 pathway can be used — document the repeat result when available.

result.redirect: Emergency Department via 111. SAH protocol: non-contrast CT Head followed by LP if CT negative, within a monitored acute setting. Do not delay for community imaging.
